PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Nov-30-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-12-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Funds Being Placed In Different Bank Accounts
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Craig Keup appeared by zoom for Antonio Smith.

Mr. Keup stated that they have no problem with the dual signature account, that they transferred a large portion of the account and that all of the funds are fully FDIC insured. Mr. Keup also stated that $420,000.00 was transferred out to Manufacturers Bank, that they left $241,000.00 in the original account, that Manufacturers Bank will put $170,000.00 in another network bank and that the funds will be fully FDIC insured. In addition, Mr. Keup stated that amount of $440,000.00 in paragraph 12 of the status report is a typo, that it should be $420,000.00 and that he filed the status petition with a hearing date of November 30, 2020. Status on the funds being placed in different bank accounts and the order to appear are off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Funds Being Placed In Different Bank Accounts
Off Calendar Jul-07-2020 Continued To Aug-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on August 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why they have not provided proof that the funds have been transferred to two other accounts so that all of the accounts are within the $250,000.00 FDIC insured amount. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-26-2020 Continued To Aug-12-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Funds Being Placed In Different Bank Accounts
Off Calendar Mar-26-2020 Continued To Jul-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Craig Keup appeared by telephone for Antonio Smith.

The Court stated that based on its calculations there should have been over $690,000.00 from the net sale proceeds, that only $661,000.00 shows up in the accounting, questions how the heir search is progressing and noted that the amount in the bank account is over the insured amount and the funds are not protected. Mr. Keup stated that the back taxes were over $20,000.00 because of re-assessment, that San Francisco went back seven years and asked for additional property taxes, and that it accounts for the difference. Mr. Keup also stated that the heir search is progressing very slowly, that they have sent letters, that they haven't gotten any verifications back and that they are still a ways away from filing a petition to determine heirship. In addition, Mr. Keup stated that separating out the bank account may take some time because it's a dual account and it requires two signatures. The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration (subject to review of the order). Status on the funds being placed in different bank accounts is set for April 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2020 Continued To Mar-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Craig Keup for Petitioner via Court Call.

Mr. Keup stated the property was sold for $750,000.00, after costs there is $661,000.00 held in a dual signature account with American Business Bank, that it has been frustrating researching the family tree, that he has a few addresses with no responses, and that he can't file tax returns without a social security number. The court noted he should file a petition to determine heirs.

The status report and petition for order approving request for additional time to file final petition for distribution is continued to March 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel to file supplemental documents, bank account statements, current and closing statements by March 3, 2020.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Continued To Feb-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Dec-27-2019 Continued To Jan-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-17-2019 Continued To Oct-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Craig Keup appeared by telephone for Antonio Smith (Petitioner).

Mr. Keup stated that they sold the property, that bond is still in place, that the proceeds from the sale are in a secured account and that the holdup is locating Heirs. Mr. Keup also stated that a Genealogist was hired, that he's still not confident that have located all of the potential Heirs and that he will file a status report by the end of this week. Status on close of estate is continued to October 15, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Keup is to have his petition for approval of report on status of administration on file by October 8, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Craig Keup for Antonio Smith (Petitioner) via Court Call

Mr. Keup stated that he given notice to all parties for which he has addresses for, worked with genealogist on family tree, and that additional address will be provided to him for people they have already identified. In addition, Mr. Keup believes the petition can go forward because all parties are protected by the bond. The Petition for Letters of Administration is Granted. Order signed in open court. Bond in the amount of $700,000 is required. Status on Close of Estate set for September 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-13-2018 Continued To Mar-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-30-2018 Continued To Feb-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-16-2018 Continued

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-24-2017 Continued To Jan-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Craig Keup appeared by telephone for Antonio Smith (Administrator).

Mr. Keup stated that he needs further time (about four months), that his Genealogist is working very hard on it and has put in over on hundred hours, that there are twelve or thirteen siblings, that they do not have a definitive, and that there are several Nephews and Nieces. Mr. Keup also stated that most of the family is back in Arkansas, that they are actively working on it and that it has been far more difficult than they anticipated. The Court informed Counsel that because his Client was not formally adopted, he has no authority and he needs to show nominations by someone with authority or give notice to the Public Guardian. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to January 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that if the search is not done by then, he needs to file a written status report.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
